name: tracing (Async-Aware Diagnostics for Rust)

performance.notes: Disabled spans have near-zero cost; enabled spans ~50ns overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Structured, async-aware logging and tracing for Rust. tracing::info!, warn!, error!, debug! macros. #[instrument] attribute for automatic span creation. Spans track async context across .await. tracing-subscriber for output (tracing_subscriber::fmt, JSON, OpenTelemetry). OpenTelemetry integration via opentelemetry-rust. Tower/axum/hyper/tokio all use tracing. Replaces log crate for async code. Structured fields: tracing::info!(user_id = %id, "request"). Used by ripgrep, tokio, axum.

gotchas: Async context propagation can be surprising across tasks
#[instrument] adds all function args to span by default (skip sensitive fields)
OpenTelemetry integration has additional setup complexity
best_when: Async Rust services needing structured logging with context
Any tokio/axum application (they use tracing internally)
Production services sending traces to OpenTelemetry/Jaeger/Zipkin
Replacing log crate in async Rust code
avoid_when: Simple sync Rust scripts (log + env_logger simpler)
No need for structured fields or async context
summary: tracing is the async-aware structured logging/tracing framework for Rust. tracing::info!, #[instrument] for auto spans, structured fields. Span context propagates across async .await. tracing-subscriber for output (fmt, JSON, OpenTelemetry). Used by axum, tokio, hyper, ripgrep internally. OpenTelemetry integration for distributed tracing. The standard instrumentation library for production async Rust.
